contract_meta.phase_name: UI Baseline Fixes - Premium UX Foundation
contract_meta.rationale: Phase A fixes the most embarrassing UI issues before building Trust Engine. Three fast wins to make system demo-ready: success card, loading states, error formatting. All frontend-only, safe changes.

project.name: executor-mvp
project.goal: Fix critical UI perception issues: raw JSON dump, static loading message, technical errors. Make system presentable before Trust Engine work.
project.scope: Wins A1-A3 only - pure frontend polish
project.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript, HTML, CSS - no backend changes, no schema changes, safe territory

execution_model.description: Agent verifies Phase 4 completion, then applies frontend polish incrementally. Test after each win.

observability.description: Continues 2025 agent observability with focus on UI improvements

tasks.title: Verify Phase 4 completion
tasks.description: Confirm Phase 4 (task planning, testing, repair) is complete before starting UI fixes

evaluation.intent: Verify Phase 4 complete before UI polish
evaluation.success_criteria: All Phase 4 artifacts exist, UI elements present, tests pass
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Phase 4 must be complete before Phase A

tasks.title: Win #A1: Success Card (Replace JSON Dump)
tasks.description: Replace raw JSON output with formatted success card showing metrics, files, and actions
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript function, HTML structure, CSS styling

implementation: Function signature: function renderSuccessCard(data)
Detect success: if (!data.ok || !data.files_written) return false
Clear resultEl first
Create div.success-card with:
- div.success-header: icon (✅) + h2 'Project Generated Successfully!'
- div.success-metrics: 3 metric cards (files, tests, time)
- div.file-list: h3 + ul of generated files with icons
- div.action-buttons: primary 'Open Project' link + secondary 'Run Tests' button
- details.raw-json: summary 'View Raw Response' + pre with JSON
Append card to resultEl
Return true to indicate success card rendered

content_spec.location: After fetch completes and data received

css: .success-card { background: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; margin-top: 16px; }
.success-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; }
.success-metrics { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr)); gap: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px; }
.metric { text-align: center; padding: 12px; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 6px; }
.metric-value { display: block; font-size: 28px; font-weight: bold; color: #a7f3d0; }
.file-list li { padding: 6px 0; color: #cbd5e1; font-family: 'Monaco', 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 14px; }
.action-buttons .btn { padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; text-decoration: none; font-weight: 500; transition: all 0.2s ease; }

success_criteria: Function called in executeRequest on success
Success card shows: header, metrics, file list, action buttons, collapsed JSON
CSS styles make card visually distinct (green theme)
JSON still available in collapsed details element

demo_requirement.manual_test: Generate a project, verify success card appears instead of raw JSON. Check metrics, file list, buttons work.
demo_requirement.approval_needed: Developer confirms success card is professional and informative

tasks.title: Win #A2: Loading States (Phase-Aware Messages)
tasks.description: Add spinner animation and phase-aware loading messages instead of static 'Planning...' text
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript timing, HTML structure, CSS animations

implementation: Add module-level variable: let loadingPhaseTimer = null;
Add module-level variable: let loadingPhase = 0;
Function signature: function updateLoadingPhase()
Phases:
Phase 0 (0-10s): 'Analyzing your request...' + 'Reading requirements and preparing plan. (typically 10-20 seconds)'
Phase 1 (10-30s): 'Creating execution plan...' + 'Breaking down into manageable steps. (typically 10-30 seconds)'
Phase 2 (30s+): 'Building your project...' + 'Generating files and running tests. This may take several minutes.'
Update resultEl with spinner HTML + phase message
Return timer reference

css: .spinner { width: 40px; height: 40px; margin: 0 auto 20px; border: 4px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3); border-top-color: #3b82f6; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite; }
.loading-hint { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; max-width: 400px; margin: 0 auto; line-height: 1.5; }

success_criteria: Three distinct phases with different messages
No visual jump or flicker between phases

demo_requirement.manual_test: Start generation, watch loading message change from phase 0 → 1 → 2. Verify spinner animates smoothly.
demo_requirement.approval_needed: Developer confirms loading states feel responsive and informative

tasks.title: Win #A3: Error Message Formatting
tasks.description: Convert technical errors into user-friendly messages with actionable guidance
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript error handling, HTML structure, CSS styling

implementation: Function signature: function formatError(error)
Define errorMap object with common errors:
'Failed to fetch': {title: 'Connection Error', message: 'Unable to connect to server', action: 'Check server is running: npm run dev'}
'ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED': {title: 'Server Not Running', message: 'Backend service not responding', action: 'Start server: npm run dev'}
'timeout': {title: 'Request Timeout', message: 'Operation took too long', action: 'Try simpler request or check logs'}
'NetworkError': {title: 'Network Error', message: 'Network connection lost', action: 'Check internet connection'}
Match error string against errorMap keys
If match found, return formatted error card HTML
If no match, return generic friendly error with technical details collapsed
HTML structure: div.error-card with header, message, action, details

css: .error-card { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; margin-top: 16px; }
.error-card .error-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 16px; }
.error-card .error-action { color: #cbd5e1; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); padding: 12px; border-radius: 6px; margin-bottom: 16px; font-family: 'Monaco', monospace; font-size: 14px; }
.error-card pre { margin-top: 12px; color: #94a3b8; font-size: 12px; max-height: 200px; overflow-y: auto; }

success_criteria: formatError function exists and handles common errors
Error card shows: title, message, actionable steps
All error paths use formatError (executeRequest, startClarificationFlow)
CSS makes errors distinct but not alarming (red theme, not aggressive)
Unknown errors still get friendly wrapping

demo_requirement.manual_test: Test errors: stop server (connection refused), cause timeout, test with invalid prompt. Verify friendly messages.
demo_requirement.approval_needed: Developer confirms errors are understandable and actionable

tasks.title: Phase A validation gate
tasks.description: Final validation that Phase A UI fixes are complete and demo-ready

actions.cmd: grep -q 'renderSuccessCard' public/script.js && grep -q 'updateLoadingPhase' public/script.js && grep -q 'formatError' public/script.js
grep -q 'success-card' public/styles.css && grep -q 'spinner' public/styles.css && grep -q 'error-card' public/styles.css

tests: Generate successful project - verify success card appears with metrics, files, buttons
Watch loading states - verify spinner animates, messages update every 10s
Stop server and try execute - verify friendly error message with connection guidance
All UI changes look professional, no raw JSON visible on success
No console errors during any flow

evaluation.intent: Confirm Phase A makes system demo-ready before Trust Engine work
evaluation.success_criteria: All UI improvements work correctly, no regressions, looks professional
evaluation.on_pass: Phase A COMPLETE - System is demo-ready. Ready for Phase B (Trust Engine)
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Fix UI issues before proceeding to Phase B

win_isolation.description: Each win must be complete, tested, and DEMOED before proceeding to next
win_isolation.implementation: Visual verification after each win. No automated tests needed for pure UI polish.

PA-GATE.on_fail: HALT execution. Do not proceed to Phase B.
PA-GATE.report: Generate diagnostic showing which UI elements failed validation.

frontend_only.enforcement: Phase A makes ZERO changes to backend, schemas, or business logic. Pure UI polish.
frontend_only.rationale: Keep UI fixes separate from Trust Engine backend work (Phase B).

fallback_policies.phase4_verification_fails: HALT - Phase 4 must be complete before Phase A.
fallback_policies.lint_fails: Fix linting errors before proceeding to next win.

release_actions.cmd: git commit -m "feat(phaseA): UI baseline fixes - success card, loading states, error formatting"

ai_executor_guidelines.observability_first: Trace all decisions. Log reasoning for each win completion.
ai_executor_guidelines.win_isolation: Complete and demo each win fully before proceeding to next. Do not mix wins.
ai_executor_guidelines.frontend_only: Phase A is frontend-only. No backend changes. No schema changes.
ai_executor_guidelines.visual_verification: After each win, verify UI changes look correct in browser.
ai_executor_guidelines.fail_fast: If any win breaks UI, halt and fix before proceeding.
ai_executor_guidelines.gate_enforcement: PA-GATE must pass before declaring phase complete.
ai_executor_guidelines.demo_requirement: Each win MUST be manually tested in browser before proceeding to next.

governance_note.for_human: Phase A is pure UI polish (safe, fast). Three wins totaling ~3 hours. After PA-GATE, system is demo-ready. No more raw JSON dumps, no more frozen loading screens, no more technical errors. Ready to show anyone without embarrassment. Then proceed to Phase B (Trust Engine) for signature moment.
